summary: High‑quality audience tape captures a stellar 1984 show with standout Help>Slip>Franklin's and Brent’s superb Friend of the Devil solo.
review: The recording offers a surprisingly clear, wide‑stereo audience capture that lets listeners enjoy a solid first set and an amazing second set highlighted by a powerhouse Help > Slip > Franklin’s, a brilliant Brent solo on Friend of the Devil, and a rousing US Blues encore. Reviewers praise both the musical performance and the tape’s low crowd noise thanks to good mic placement.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed positive review highlights an excellent performance and a good‑quality audience source, justifying a rating slightly above the reviewer’s 4‑star score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Wide stereo field with microphones positioned above the audience to limit crowd noise; analog master preferred over grainy 14‑bit PCM; overall clear and listenable for an audience tape.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong, cohesive first set that builds momentum into an even more exciting second set with superb transitions and a celebratory encore.

band_member_comments.Keys: Brent delivers what the reviewer calls the best solo they've heard on Friend of the Devil.
band_member_comments.Drums: Powerful pre‑drums segment in Help>Slip>Franklin's and overall strong drumming throughout the show.
